claimText: Bonobos are endemic exclusively to the forests of the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC).
evidenceExcerpt: Found only in the forests of the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC).

claimText: Bonobos are characterized by a peaceful social structure with female-led groups.
evidenceExcerpt: They are peaceful, female-led, and smaller/darker than chimpanzees.
examUse: Statement-based question comparing Bonobo and Chimpanzee social structures.

claimText: Bonobos share 98.7% of their DNA with humans, making them one of our closest living relatives alongside chimpanzees.
evidenceExcerpt: Like Chimpanzees, they share 98.7% of DNA with humans.
examUse: Statement-based question on evolutionary biology and primates.
distractorHooks: Are not closely related to humans

claimText: The conservation status of the Bonobo is Endangered on the IUCN Red List and it is listed under CITES Appendix I.
evidenceExcerpt: Conservation Status: CITES Appendix I, IUCN Endangered.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching species to IUCN status.

claimText: Vocal communication among Bonobos exhibits compositionality, a trait similar to human language.
evidenceExcerpt: A study found that vocal communication between Bonobos shows compositionality, similar to human language.
examUse: Statement-based question on animal cognition and communication.
